discovery.run_summary: Risk-off tone with materials (XLB -2.35%), financials, and homebuilder-adjacent industrials underperforming as 30-yr yields hit 5.19% and mortgage rates surge. Energy and defensives (XLV/XLU) catch a bid amid Iran tensions. Mid-cap movers concentrated in rate-sensitive building products (BLDR) and semicap (ONTO).

notable_index_moves: XLB -2.35% leads downside, consistent with rates shock hitting cyclicals/housing
XLE +1.12% and XLU +0.91% bid on Iran/defensive rotation
30-yr Treasury yield 5.19% — highest since pre-GFC; mortgage rates near YTD high
VIX +1.4% to 18 — elevated but not panicked

discoveries.name: Builders FirstSource, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Officer departure 8-K filed into a sharply deteriorating rates/housing backdrop
discoveries.thesis: BLDR filed a multi-item 8-K including 5.02 (officer departure) and 7.01 (Reg FD) on the same day mortgage rates spiked toward 7% and 30-yr yields hit a multi-decade high. The combination of company-specific management change PLUS a macro housing-demand shock is a classic bad-news bundle for a homebuilder supplier; the multi-day decline suggests selling is ongoing rather than capitulated, and the Reg FD item raises odds of negative guidance commentary. Lean bearish for continuation.
discoveries.what_confirms: 8-K exhibit reveals CFO or COO departure (vs. routine board change), Reg FD disclosure contains softer volume/pricing commentary, and mortgage rates continue higher this week
discoveries.what_kills: 5.02 turns out to be a planned retirement with named successor, 7.01 exhibit is benign (e.g., investor day reaffirmation), or rates reverse sharply on a dovish Fed signal
discoveries.what_to_learn: 5.02 + 7.01 combo filings during macro stress for rate-sensitive names deserve a read of the actual exhibit before assuming routine — Reg FD on the same day as a personnel change frequently means management is pre-empting a guide-down
discoveries.catalyst: Multi-item 8-K (5.02 officer change + 7.01 Reg FD) coinciding with 30-yr yield spike and mortgage rate surge
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 8-K items 5.02 and 7.01 filed together is the pattern that often signals coordinated bad-news bundling; macro tape (mortgage rates highest since July) compounds the housing-demand fear
research_pointers: Pull the 8-K exhibit to identify which officer departed and whether a successor was named
Check the 7.01 attachment for any guidance/volume commentary
Compare BLDR's move vs. peers (LPX, BCC, EXP) to isolate company-specific vs. macro component
Map BLDR EV/EBITDA against 2018-2019 rate-shock comp to gauge downside if mortgage rates stick above 7%

discoveries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
discoveries.setup: Semicap extending decline on a vague 8.01 catch-all 8-K with no item code clarity
discoveries.thesis: ONTO filed an 8.01 'other events' 8-K which is the lowest-signal item code, and the stock has continued lower over several sessions on heavy volume (4x average). Without the exhibit content, I cannot tell if this is a guide-down disclosure, a customer-loss notice, or boilerplate. SMH is only modestly down today, so this is company-specific, but I cannot articulate a confident direction either way from item 8.01 alone.
discoveries.catalyst: 8-K item 8.01 (other events) filed May 18; multi-day decline on elevated volume
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Item 8.01 is a catch-all; cannot infer direction from code alone, and no other catalyst signals (no earnings window flagged)
research_pointers: Read the actual 8.01 exhibit — likely contains the substantive disclosure
Cross-check against KLAC/AMAT/LRCX moves to confirm idiosyncratic vs. semicap-wide weakness
Check for any HBM/advanced-packaging capex commentary from customers (Micron, SK Hynix) over the past week

discoveries.name: Repligen Corporation
discoveries.setup: Bioprocessing name bouncing on a routine annual-meeting filing in a defensive-bid tape
discoveries.thesis: RGEN's 8-K is item 5.07 (typically annual meeting voting results), which is administrative and not a catalyst. The move is consistent with XLV +1.1% defensive rotation and a bounce off a recent multi-day decline. No clear mispricing — magnitude is appropriate given sector rotation and recent weakness.
discoveries.what_confirms: Move fades if XLV bid fades tomorrow
discoveries.what_kills: 8-K exhibit contains something other than vote results (unlikely for 5.07-only)
discoveries.catalyst: Defensive sector rotation + technical bounce; 5.07 filing is non-substantive
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Standalone 5.07 filing is almost always annual meeting results — non-market-moving
research_pointers: Confirm 5.07 exhibit is just voting results
Check whether bioprocessing peers (DHR life sciences, TMO, SRT) are also bouncing

discoveries.name: SkyWest, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Regional airline weak with no SEC catalyst and no earnings window
discoveries.thesis: SKYW has continued lower over multiple sessions with no 8-K, no recent or upcoming earnings, and on below-average volume. Most likely driver is jet fuel pressure from XLE/oil bid on Iran tensions and broader risk-off cyclical selling, but with no idiosyncratic signal I can't make a directional call at conf 3+.
discoveries.catalyst: No company-specific catalyst; likely sympathy with oil-price-driven airline weakness
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals; move likely macro/sector sympathy with rising crude on Iran headlines
research_pointers: Check jet fuel crack spreads and crude price action this week
Compare to ALK, ALGT, MESA for sector-wide vs. company-specific divergence

ai_announcements.headline: Google I/O 2026 megadrop: Gemini 3.5 Flash, agents, AI Search overhaul, Universal Cart, Antigravity 2.0, AI design, Android app gen, Gemini Spark
ai_announcements.summary: Google made a sweeping push into agentic AI: agents replacing chatbots, AI-first Search (further squeezing publisher referrals), Universal Cart for cross-site shopping, AI design tools, AI-generated Android apps, and a $100 AI Ultra tier. Also: Karpathy joins Anthropic pre-training; SandboxAQ integrates with Claude.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Onto sells metrology/inspection equipment to fabs; AI buildout INCREASES demand for advanced-node wafer inspection. Today's move is more likely sympathy with semi-equipment weakness or the 8-K (item 8.01 unspecified) than Google I/O.
research_pointers: Read 5/18 8-K item 8.01 content - likely the real catalyst, not AI news
Check semi-equipment peer moves (KLAC, CAMT, ACMR) for sector vs idiosyncratic
Onto exposure to HBM/advanced packaging - direct AI capex beneficiary

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Builders FirstSource sells lumber/building products to homebuilders - no AI substitution risk. 8-K shows officer change (5.02) + annual meeting (5.07) + Reg FD - this is the catalyst, not Google I/O. Housing/rates macro also weighing.
research_pointers: Read 5/18 8-K - officer departure detail is likely the real driver
Check homebuilder peers (DHI, LEN) and lumber prices
Not an AI story - exclude from AI selloff thesis

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Regional airline - zero AI overlap. Move is airline-sector or fuel/macro driven.
research_pointers: Check airline sector (ALK, UAL) and oil prices
Not AI-related

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Repligen makes bioprocessing tools; SandboxAQ-Claude drug discovery integration is tangential. Up move on 1.57x volume likely post-annual-meeting (5.07 filing) or bioprocessing sector, not AI.
research_pointers: Check bioprocessing peers (DHR, SRT)
Annual meeting vote outcome from 5/15 8-K

ai_announcements.headline: Karpathy joins Anthropic pre-training team; SandboxAQ brings drug discovery to Claude
ai_announcements.summary: Anthropic-related: ANTHROPIC-RELATED CALL - flagged for bias grading. High-profile Karpathy hire signals Anthropic doubling down on frontier pre-training. SandboxAQ partnership extends Claude into pharma R&D workflows. No directly affected public stocks in today's mover list.

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: None of the four moving stocks in today's market_data (ONTO, RGEN, BLDR, SKYW) have plausible direct linkage to today's AI announcements. BLDR and SKYW are clearly non-AI (housing/airlines). ONTO and RGEN have idiosyncratic 8-K catalysts that better explain their moves. The Google I/O megadrop has real implications for AI-adjacent names (publisher/SEO stocks, Shopify/PYPL on Universal Cart, Figma/ADBE on AI design, Adobe/Canva on app generation, NVDA on AI Ultra spend) but none appear in today's mover set. Quiet day for the AI-selloff thesis from this watchlist.
